title: Extensions – LiteLLM & AnyLLM Models

classes.name: Model
methods: get_response(system_instructions, input, model_settings, tools, output_schema, handoffs, tracing, *, previous_response_id, conversation_id, prompt) -> ModelResponse [abstract]

stream_response(system_instructions, input, model_settings, tools, output_schema, handoffs, tracing, *, previous_response_id, conversation_id, prompt) -> AsyncIterator[TResponseStreamEvent] [abstract]

classes.name: LitellmModel
classes.docstring: Enables using any model via LiteLLM (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Mistral, etc.)
methods: _fetch_response(..., stream: bool) -> ModelResponse | tuple[Response, AsyncStream]  [calls litellm.acompletion()]

classes.name: LitellmProvider
classes.docstring: Routes model calls through LiteLLM. API keys set via env vars.
methods: get_model(model_name: str | None) -> LitellmModel  [returns LitellmModel(model_name or get_default_model())]

classes.name: LitellmConverter
methods: convert_message_to_openai(message: litellm.Message, model: str | None) -> ChatCompletionMessage  [classmethod, produces InternalChatCompletionMessage]
convert_tool_call_to_openai(tool_call: litellm.ChatCompletionMessageToolCall, model: str | None) -> ChatCompletionMessageFunctionToolCall  [classmethod, may produce InternalToolCall]

classes.name: InternalChatCompletionMessage
classes.purpose: Carries reasoning_content and thinking_blocks without modifying the original OpenAI model

classes.name: InternalToolCall
classes.purpose: Carries provider-specific metadata (e.g., Gemini thought signatures)

classes.name: AnyLLMModel
classes.docstring: Routes model calls through any-llm SDK. Supports both 'responses' and 'chat_completions' APIs depending on provider capability.

classes.name: AnyLLMProvider
classes.docstring: Routes model calls through any-llm. API keys from provider-specific env vars.
methods: get_model(model_name: str | None) -> AnyLLMModel  [returns AnyLLMModel(model_name or DEFAULT_MODEL, api_key, base_url, api)]

classes.name: _AnyLLMResponsesParamsShim
classes.purpose: Fallback when any-llm types not importable; wraps payload dict and exposes model_dump()

key_relationships: LitellmModel extends Model (implements abstract get_response, stream_response)
LitellmModel uses LitellmConverter (composition — calls convert_message_to_openai in get_response)
AnyLLMModel extends Model (implements abstract get_response, stream_response)
AnyLLMModel depends_on Converter (chatcmpl_converter) for message conversion
AnyLLMModel depends_on ChatCmplStreamHandler for stream processing
AnyLLMModel depends_on OpenAIResponsesConverter for responses API path
LitellmModel depends_on Converter (chatcmpl_converter) for message conversion
LitellmModel depends_on ChatCmplStreamHandler for stream processing
Both LitellmModel and AnyLLMModel use generation_span / response_span for tracing
behavioral_notes: LitellmModel always routes via litellm.acompletion() (chat_completions path only)
AnyLLMModel auto-selects API: uses 'responses' if provider.SUPPORTS_RESPONSES, else 'chat_completions'
AnyLLMModel model name must use 'provider/model' format (e.g. 'openai/gpt-4.1'). Bare name defaults to 'openai' prefix
AnyLLMProvider constructor accepts api, api_key, base_url; LitellmProvider takes no constructor args
LitellmModel fixes tool message ordering for Anthropic/Claude/Gemini providers
LitellmModel converts Gemini extra_content (thought_signature) to provider_specific_fields
Both models support reasoning_effort forwarding via ModelSettings
Provider-managed retries can be disabled via should_disable_provider_managed_retries()
InternalChatCompletionMessage carries reasoning_content + thinking_blocks for extended thinking (Anthropic Claude)
InternalToolCall carries extra_content for Gemini thought signatures
important_notes: LitellmProvider default model is 'gpt-4.1'. AnyLLMProvider DEFAULT_MODEL is 'openai/{get_default_model()}'. Both providers are used via RunConfig(model_provider=...). LiteLLM serializer warnings can be patched via OPENAI_AGENTS_ENABLE_LITELLM_SERIALIZER_PATCH=true env var.
